reasoning: The Lean statement encodes the claim that 1729 is the minimum (via sInf over ℕ, which gives the least element when the set is nonempty per Nat.sInf_def) positive integer expressible as x³+y³ = z³+w³ with positive naturals and disjoint representations. The many disequalities (x≠z, x≠w, y≠z, y≠w) seem stronger than "two different representations", but for positive integer cubes summing to the same value, if the unordered pairs share any element then they must be equal (since a=c forces b=d), so disjointness is equivalent to being distinct unordered pairs — exactly the informal meaning of "two ways". The use of ℕ with positivity (instead of ℤ for "integral") makes explicit the implicit positive-integer premise that is required for the statement to be true (otherwise 91 = 3³+4³ = 6³+(-5)³ would be a counterexample), which is the standard Hardy–Ramanujan reading of the taxicab problem. This is faithful surfacing of an implicit premise, not a weakening.
